questions.query: What is the purpose of Managing Public Money?
questions.full_answer:
It may be that the nature of the body is such that it 
would be appropriate to consider if that a requirement for compliance 
with the principles of Managing Public Money should be imposed. Public corporations do not have accounting officers and are not subject 
to Managing Public Money as a matter of course. Unlike Public Corporations in 
general, trading funds have accounting officers appointed by the 
Treasury and are subject to Managing Public Money. 8 
Chapter 1 
Responsibilities 
 
 
1.1 M

questions.query: Who is accountable for public spending decisions?
questions.full_answer:
Departments may pay them 
grants (for specific purposes) and grants-in-aid (non-specific support) to 
finance their spending; though it is the net spending of the ALB that 
scores in the departmental budget. Each covers the net expenditure 
of a department and its ALBs (that is all spending in budgets and any 
voted spend outside of budgets) for the year ahead. Where the expenditure covers multiple years 
within a spending review period, the Treasury expects accounting 
officers to ensure budget

questions.query: What approvals are needed before committing public funds?
questions.full_answer:
2.3.11 Where proposals have public expenditure implications, the 
Treasury should be consulted before they are submitted for approval by 
collective agreement at cabinet level, either in person, by write-round 
or via a cabinet sub-committee. 2.3.10 The Treasury approval process may also include requirements to 
obtain approvals from other government departments or bodies in 
relation to specific types of expenditure, for example Cabinet Office 
commercial controls. Failure to obtain such approv
questions.datter_answer:
Most trading funds are public corporations, but some may be 
central government companies. Unlike Public Corporations in 
general, trading funds have accounting officers appointed by the 
Treasury and are subject to Managing Public Money. Public corporations do not have accounting officers and are not subject 
to Managing Public Money as a matter of course. Box 4.4 Essentials of systems for committing and 
paying funds

questions.query: How should irregularities or fraud be handled?
questions.full_answer:
168 
A4.9.5 Each organisation shall identify and assess at different levels how 
it might be vulnerable to fraud with reference to the government 
standards for Fraud Risk Assessment. Fraud should be always 
considered as a risk for the departments’ risk register. The Fraud Act 2006 recognises that a criminal offence of fraud arises 
from causing a loss to an individual or legal entity through the 
intentional misdeclaration of information; knowingly withholding 
information; or through an abuse
questions.datter_answer:
168

questions.random_answer:
Fraud should be always 
considered as a risk for the departments’ risk register. Responding to fraud risk 
A4.9.7 The organisation’s response to fraud risk should be customised 
to the risks it faces. Typically it will involve some or all of the following: 
• Developing a Fraud Policy Statement, a Fraud Risk 
Strategy and a Fraud Response Plan (key documents that 
every organisation should have). Assurances about these measures can 
be obtained from Internal Audit fraud loss measurement exercise

questions.query: What role do internal controls play in public finance?
questions.full_answer:
Box A4.1A The role of the senior officer responsible for 
finance  
Governance:  
● financial leadership, both within the organisation and to its 
ALBs, at both a strategic and operational level 
● ensuring sound and appropriate financial governance and 
financial risk management  
● leading, motivating and developing the finance function, 
establishing its full commercial contribution to the business 
● planning and delivering the financial framework agreed with 
the Treasury or sponsoring orga
